technique: Impossible travel - one identity authenticating from geographically impossible locations
tactic: TA0001 Initial Access -> TA0005 Defense Evasion

trigger.from_breadth: A UPN with SUCCESSFUL sign-ins from >=2 distinct countries in the window, OR a credential-stuffing-pivot / password-spray lead whose success came from a new country (UEBA new-geo signal).

telemetry_gap_behavior: If country fields are null but source_ip exists, resolve country from the IP (geo lookup / raw message) before scoring. If neither country nor IP is present on the success events, emit REVIEW geo-blind for the UPN - NEVER clean off a null geo field. If the Azure stream total=0/window, emit REVIEW stream-empty.

method.goal: Pull every SUCCESS sign-in for the UPN across a 12h look-back (the prior leg may predate the hourly window)
method.compute: ordered-by-time list of {timestamp, source_ip, country, city, geo_lat_long, appDisplayName, clientAppUsed}

method.goal: Compute required travel speed between consecutive logins
method.compute: for each consecutive pair: great-circle km between geos / hours between timestamps = required km/h. Track distinct countries and the minimum inter-country time gap.
method.decision_input: required speed > 900 km/h (faster than commercial air) = IMPOSSIBLE; country change with < 60 min gap and > 1000 km = impossible

method.goal: Grade token / MFA posture of the suspect leg (replay vs fresh auth)
method.fields: properties_authenticationRequirement (singleFactor vs multiFactor) ; properties_incomingTokenType (primaryRefreshToken = PRT/replay) ; properties_tokenProtectionStatusDetails_signInSessionStatus (unbound = AiTM) ; properties_conditionalAccessStatus OR azure_prob_condi_access_status
method.decision_input: impossible-travel leg that is singleFactor where MFA is required, OR an unbound/PRT token from the new geo = token replay / AiTM, not just a second password auth

method.check: clientAppUsed of the suspect leg in {Authenticated SMTP, BAV2ROPC, IMAP4, POP3, Exchange ActiveSync, Other clients}; appDisplayName of both legs
method.decision_input: legacy clientApp on the foreign leg raises ceiling (MFA-bypass capable)

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: impossible-speed pair of SUCCESSES AND (foreign leg is singleFactor/legacy/unbound-token OR the foreign country is outside the GEO-ACL)
decision.HIGH_conf4: 2+ countries for the UPN within the window with a borderline-impossible gap, MFA satisfied but from a never-before-seen country (new-geo)
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: 2 countries but one leg is a known VPN/Cato egress or a mobile-carrier CGNAT flip - report as review-worthy, not confirmed
decision.FP_kill: the second country is a Cato egress IP (140.82.202.196 / 199.27.40.187 / 123.253.153.138) or a documented corporate VPN; both legs MFA-satisfied from the user's baseline devices

false_positive_checks: Cato egress 140.82.202.196 / 199.27.40.187 / 123.253.153.138 -> country flip is egress, not travel; cap MEDIUM unless paired with admin/export action
corporate VPN / cloud-proxy egress that legitimately changes apparent country
mobile carrier CGNAT that geolocates to a neighboring country
known traveler baseline for the UPN (if a 7-day baseline of countries is available)
